title: [lichen_l2.c:1609-1621] SECURITY: DEBUG logging exposes full EUI-64 addresses
description:
Security finding: At DEBUG log level, full peer EUI-64 addresses are logged (lines 1609-1621, 552-556).

The comment at lines 1607-1617 justifies this as acceptable because:
1. DEBUG requires CONFIG_LICHEN_L2_LOG_LEVEL=4
2. EUI-64 is 'already exposed in IEEE 802.15.4-style link-layer addresses'

However:
1. Production builds may accidentally enable DEBUG level
2. Log files may be exfiltrated or stored insecurely
3. Correlating EUI-64 to physical device location enables tracking
4. Mesh topology can be reconstructed from DEBUG logs

Impact: P2 - privacy leak, not direct security exploit. Requires DEBUG level enabled.

Mitigation:
1. Consider truncating EUI-64 in logs (first 4 bytes only)
2. Add compile-time warning when DEBUG level is enabled
3. Or: keep current behavior but document privacy implications
close_reason: Documented: EUI-64 logging at DEBUG level is accepted risk. Requires explicit CONFIG_LICHEN_L2_LOG_LEVEL=4, logs authenticated peers only, EUI-64 already exposed in link-layer addresses.
